title: BlackSky Technology Full Year 2024 Earnings: Misses Expectations
text: BlackSky Technology (NYSE: BKSY) reported its 2024 full-year financial results, which fell short of analyst expectations. The company generated Revenue: US$102.1m (up 8.0% from FY 2023) while posting a Net loss: US$57.2m (loss widened by 6.2% from FY 2023). The per-share loss reached $2.67. Both top and bottom-line results underwhelmed investors. Revenue missed analyst estimates by 3.6%, while earnings per share significantly underperformed, missing forecasts by 32%. The North America segment drove the majority of revenue, contributing $63.8 million, representing 62% of total sales. General and administrative expenses constituted the largest cost category at $73.0 million, or 55% of overall operating expenses. Management projects revenue growth will accelerate to 22% annually over the next three years, considerably outpacing the Professional Services industry's forecasted 6.4% expansion rate. The stock declined 4.1% in the week following the earnings announcement. Analysts have flagged three warning signs for potential investors, with one flagged as particularly concerning.
